ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 178862, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_master_src.master_assets  (900 rows)

Columns:
- `asset_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `asset_name` text NULL
- `serien_nummer` text NULL
- `garantieende` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `projekt_kennung` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_kontakte  (497 rows)

Columns:
- `kontakt_id` text NOT NULL
- `rufname` text NULL
- `familienname` text NULL
- `kontakt_email` text NULL
- `tel` text NULL
- `berufsbezeichnung` text NULL
- `rolle` text NULL
- `korrespondenzsprache` text NULL
- `kd_nummer` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_kunden  (210 rows)

Columns:
- `kundennummer` text NOT NULL
- `unternehmensname` text NULL
- `erp_nr` text NULL
- `kundenklasse` text NULL
- `vertriebsgebiet` text NULL
- `industrie` text NULL
- `homepage` text NULL
- `stadt` text NULL
- `land_region` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_opportunities  (625 rows)

Columns:
- `opp_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `titel` text NULL
- `vertriebsphase` text NULL
- `zieldatum` text NULL
- `auftragswert` text NULL
- `waehrungscode` text NULL
- `kunden_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_projekte  (342 rows)

Columns:
- `projekt_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `projektname` text NULL
- `projektstatus` text NULL
- `go_live_datum` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `opp_kennung_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)
- `Region__c` text
- `Industry` text
- `Website` text
- `BillingCity` text
- `BillingCountry` text
- `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Contact
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `FirstName` text
- `LastName` text NOT NULL
- `Email` text
- `Phone` text
- `Title` text
- `Role__c` text in (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor)
- `Preferred_Language__c` text in (DE, EN, FR, ES, IT)
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Opportunity
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `StageName` text NOT NULL in (Prospecting, Qualification, Needs Analysis, Value Proposition, Id. Decision Makers, Perception Analysis, Proposal/Price Quote, Negotiation/Review, Closed Won, Closed Lost)
- `CloseDate` text NOT NULL
- `Amount` double precision
- `CurrencyIsoCode` text
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Project__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Project_Status__c` text in (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled)
- `Go_Live_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Opportunity__c` text
- `Legacy_Project_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Installed_Asset__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Serial_Number__c` text
- `Warranty_End_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text
- `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Produce the dbt project that transforms the source into the target.

Self-correction is ON: write complete models; the orchestrator will run dbt after you finish and prompt you to fix failures if needed.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 178867, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 179014, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f0f14-3c12-759d-8f76-04bb37509b7d', conversation_id='019f0f14-3c12-759d-8f76-04ba2cd5cf4f')

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    kundennummer AS "Id",
    COALESCE(unternehmensname, kundennummer) AS "Name",
    erp_nr AS "ERP_Number__c",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(kundenklasse) = 'gold' THEN 'Gold'
        WHEN LOWER(kundenklasse) = 'silver' THEN 'Silver'
        WHEN LOWER(kundenklasse) = 'bronze' THEN 'Bronze'
        WHEN LOWER(kundenklasse) IN ('platinum', 'platin') THEN 'Platinum'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Customer_Tier__c",
    vertriebsgebiet AS "Region__c",
    industrie AS "Industry",
    homepage AS "Website",
    stadt AS "BillingCity",
    land_region AS "BillingCountry",
    kundennummer AS "Legacy_Customer_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kunden') }}
'}, tool_call_id='pyd_ai_3ca4af5e065c4fb7a8d4a28ea8927f7c', provider_name='google-cloud', provider_details={'thought_signature': ''})], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=3405, cache_read_tokens=2578, output_tokens=403, details={'cached_content_tokens': 2578, 'thoughts_tokens': 140, 'text_prompt_tokens': 3405, 'text_cache_tokens': 2578, 'text_candidates_tokens': 263}), model_name='gemini-2.5-flash', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 41, 895844, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='google-cloud', provider_url='https://aiplatform.googleapis.com/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'STOP', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 38, 82590, tzinfo=TzInfo(0)), 'traffic_type': 'ON_DEMAND'}, provider_response_id='4kxBap6FBeu5vdIP6MXqiQE', finish_reason='stop')

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 178862, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 178862, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_master_src.master_assets  (900 rows)

Columns:
- `asset_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `asset_name` text NULL
- `serien_nummer` text NULL
- `garantieende` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `projekt_kennung` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_kontakte  (497 rows)

Columns:
- `kontakt_id` text NOT NULL
- `rufname` text NULL
- `familienname` text NULL
- `kontakt_email` text NULL
- `tel` text NULL
- `berufsbezeichnung` text NULL
- `rolle` text NULL
- `korrespondenzsprache` text NULL
- `kd_nummer` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_kunden  (210 rows)

Columns:
- `kundennummer` text NOT NULL
- `unternehmensname` text NULL
- `erp_nr` text NULL
- `kundenklasse` text NULL
- `vertriebsgebiet` text NULL
- `industrie` text NULL
- `homepage` text NULL
- `stadt` text NULL
- `land_region` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_opportunities  (625 rows)

Columns:
- `opp_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `titel` text NULL
- `vertriebsphase` text NULL
- `zieldatum` text NULL
- `auftragswert` text NULL
- `waehrungscode` text NULL
- `kunden_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_projekte  (342 rows)

Columns:
- `projekt_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `projektname` text NULL
- `projektstatus` text NULL
- `go_live_datum` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `opp_kennung_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)
- `Region__c` text
- `Industry` text
- `Website` text
- `BillingCity` text
- `BillingCountry` text
- `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Contact
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `FirstName` text
- `LastName` text NOT NULL
- `Email` text
- `Phone` text
- `Title` text
- `Role__c` text in (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor)
- `Preferred_Language__c` text in (DE, EN, FR, ES, IT)
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Opportunity
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `StageName` text NOT NULL in (Prospecting, Qualification, Needs Analysis, Value Proposition, Id. Decision Makers, Perception Analysis, Proposal/Price Quote, Negotiation/Review, Closed Won, Closed Lost)
- `CloseDate` text NOT NULL
- `Amount` double precision
- `CurrencyIsoCode` text
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Project__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Project_Status__c` text in (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled)
- `Go_Live_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Opportunity__c` text
- `Legacy_Project_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Installed_Asset__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Serial_Number__c` text
- `Warranty_End_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text
- `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Produce the dbt project that transforms the source into the target.

Self-correction is ON: write complete models; the orchestrator will run dbt after you finish and prompt you to fix failures if needed.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 178867, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 179014, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f0f14-3c12-759d-8f76-04bb37509b7d', conversation_id='019f0f14-3c12-759d-8f76-04ba2cd5cf4f')

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 178862, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_master_src.master_assets  (900 rows)

Columns:
- `asset_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `asset_name` text NULL
- `serien_nummer` text NULL
- `garantieende` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `projekt_kennung` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_kontakte  (497 rows)

Columns:
- `kontakt_id` text NOT NULL
- `rufname` text NULL
- `familienname` text NULL
- `kontakt_email` text NULL
- `tel` text NULL
- `berufsbezeichnung` text NULL
- `rolle` text NULL
- `korrespondenzsprache` text NULL
- `kd_nummer` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_kunden  (210 rows)

Columns:
- `kundennummer` text NOT NULL
- `unternehmensname` text NULL
- `erp_nr` text NULL
- `kundenklasse` text NULL
- `vertriebsgebiet` text NULL
- `industrie` text NULL
- `homepage` text NULL
- `stadt` text NULL
- `land_region` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_opportunities  (625 rows)

Columns:
- `opp_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `titel` text NULL
- `vertriebsphase` text NULL
- `zieldatum` text NULL
- `auftragswert` text NULL
- `waehrungscode` text NULL
- `kunden_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_projekte  (342 rows)

Columns:
- `projekt_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `projektname` text NULL
- `projektstatus` text NULL
- `go_live_datum` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `opp_kennung_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)
- `Region__c` text
- `Industry` text
- `Website` text
- `BillingCity` text
- `BillingCountry` text
- `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Contact
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `FirstName` text
- `LastName` text NOT NULL
- `Email` text
- `Phone` text
- `Title` text
- `Role__c` text in (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor)
- `Preferred_Language__c` text in (DE, EN, FR, ES, IT)
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Opportunity
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `StageName` text NOT NULL in (Prospecting, Qualification, Needs Analysis, Value Proposition, Id. Decision Makers, Perception Analysis, Proposal/Price Quote, Negotiation/Review, Closed Won, Closed Lost)
- `CloseDate` text NOT NULL
- `Amount` double precision
- `CurrencyIsoCode` text
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Project__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Project_Status__c` text in (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled)
- `Go_Live_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Opportunity__c` text
- `Legacy_Project_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Installed_Asset__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Serial_Number__c` text
- `Warranty_End_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text
- `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Produce the dbt project that transforms the source into the target.

Self-correction is ON: write complete models; the orchestrator will run dbt after you finish and prompt you to fix failures if needed.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 178867, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 179014, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f0f14-3c12-759d-8f76-04bb37509b7d', conversation_id='019f0f14-3c12-759d-8f76-04ba2cd5cf4f')

    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM

ModelResponse(parts=[ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args={'content': '-- models/Contact.sql

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    kontakt_id AS "Id",
    rufname AS "FirstName",
    COALESCE(familienname, 'Unknown') AS "LastName",
    kontakt_email AS "Email",
    tel AS "Phone",
    berufsbezeichnung AS "Title",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(rolle) IN ('decision maker', 'entscheider') THEN 'Decision Maker'
        WHEN LOWER(rolle) = 'end user' THEN 'End User'
        WHEN LOWER(rolle) = 'technical contact' THEN 'Technical Contact'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Role__c",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(korrespondenzsprache) IN ('de', 'deutsch') THEN 'DE'
        WHEN LOWER(korrespondenzsprache) IN ('en', 'english', 'englisch') THEN 'EN'
        WHEN LOWER(korrespondenzsprache) = 'fr' THEN 'FR'
        WHEN LOWER(korrespondenzsprache) = 'es' THEN 'ES'
        WHEN LOWER(korrespondenzsprache) = 'it' THEN 'IT'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Preferred_Language__c",
    kd_nummer AS "AccountId",
    kontakt_id AS "Legacy_Contact_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 178862, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 178862, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_master_src.master_assets  (900 rows)

Columns:
- `asset_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `asset_name` text NULL
- `serien_nummer` text NULL
- `garantieende` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `projekt_kennung` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_kontakte  (497 rows)

Columns:
- `kontakt_id` text NOT NULL
- `rufname` text NULL
- `familienname` text NULL
- `kontakt_email` text NULL
- `tel` text NULL
- `berufsbezeichnung` text NULL
- `rolle` text NULL
- `korrespondenzsprache` text NULL
- `kd_nummer` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_kunden  (210 rows)

Columns:
- `kundennummer` text NOT NULL
- `unternehmensname` text NULL
- `erp_nr` text NULL
- `kundenklasse` text NULL
- `vertriebsgebiet` text NULL
- `industrie` text NULL
- `homepage` text NULL
- `stadt` text NULL
- `land_region` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_opportunities  (625 rows)

Columns:
- `opp_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `titel` text NULL
- `vertriebsphase` text NULL
- `zieldatum` text NULL
- `auftragswert` text NULL
- `waehrungscode` text NULL
- `kunden_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_projekte  (342 rows)

Columns:
- `projekt_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `projektname` text NULL
- `projektstatus` text NULL
- `go_live_datum` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `opp_kennung_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)
- `Region__c` text
- `Industry` text
- `Website` text
- `BillingCity` text
- `BillingCountry` text
- `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Contact
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `FirstName` text
- `LastName` text NOT NULL
- `Email` text
- `Phone` text
- `Title` text
- `Role__c` text in (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor)
- `Preferred_Language__c` text in (DE, EN, FR, ES, IT)
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Opportunity
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `StageName` text NOT NULL in (Prospecting, Qualification, Needs Analysis, Value Proposition, Id. Decision Makers, Perception Analysis, Proposal/Price Quote, Negotiation/Review, Closed Won, Closed Lost)
- `CloseDate` text NOT NULL
- `Amount` double precision
- `CurrencyIsoCode` text
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Project__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Project_Status__c` text in (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled)
- `Go_Live_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Opportunity__c` text
- `Legacy_Project_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Installed_Asset__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Serial_Number__c` text
- `Warranty_End_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text
- `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Produce the dbt project that transforms the source into the target.

Self-correction is ON: write complete models; the orchestrator will run dbt after you finish and prompt you to fix failures if needed.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 178867, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 179014, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f0f14-3c12-759d-8f76-04bb37509b7d', conversation_id='019f0f14-3c12-759d-8f76-04ba2cd5cf4f')

ModelResponse(parts=[ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args={'path': 'models/Account.sql', 'content': '-- models/Account.sql

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    kundennummer AS "Id",
    COALESCE(unternehmensname, kundennummer) AS "Name",
    erp_nr AS "ERP_Number__c",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(kundenklasse) = 'gold' THEN 'Gold'
        WHEN LOWER(kundenklasse) = 'silver' THEN 'Silver'
        WHEN LOWER(kundenklasse) = 'bronze' THEN 'Bronze'
        WHEN LOWER(kundenklasse) IN ('platinum', 'platin') THEN 'Platinum'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Customer_Tier__c",
    vertriebsgebiet AS "Region__c",
    industrie AS "Industry",
    homepage AS "Website",
    stadt AS "BillingCity",
    land_region AS "BillingCountry",
    kundennummer AS "Legacy_Customer_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kunden') }}
'}, tool_call_id='pyd_ai_3ca4af5e065c4fb7a8d4a28ea8927f7c', provider_name='google-cloud', provider_details={'thought_signature': ''})], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=3405, cache_read_tokens=2578, output_tokens=403, details={'cached_content_tokens': 2578, 'thoughts_tokens': 140, 'text_prompt_tokens': 3405, 'text_cache_tokens': 2578, 'text_candidates_tokens': 263}), model_name='gemini-2.5-flash', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 41, 895844, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='google-cloud', provider_url='https://aiplatform.googleapis.com/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'STOP', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 38, 82590, tzinfo=TzInfo(0)), 'traffic_type': 'ON_DEMAND'}, provider_response_id='4kxBap6FBeu5vdIP6MXqiQE', finish_reason='stop', run_id='019f0f14-3c12-759d-8f76-04bb37509b7d', conversation_id='019f0f14-3c12-759d-8f76-04ba2cd5cf4f')

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 178862, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_master_src.master_assets  (900 rows)

Columns:
- `asset_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `asset_name` text NULL
- `serien_nummer` text NULL
- `garantieende` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `projekt_kennung` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_kontakte  (497 rows)

Columns:
- `kontakt_id` text NOT NULL
- `rufname` text NULL
- `familienname` text NULL
- `kontakt_email` text NULL
- `tel` text NULL
- `berufsbezeichnung` text NULL
- `rolle` text NULL
- `korrespondenzsprache` text NULL
- `kd_nummer` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_kunden  (210 rows)

Columns:
- `kundennummer` text NOT NULL
- `unternehmensname` text NULL
- `erp_nr` text NULL
- `kundenklasse` text NULL
- `vertriebsgebiet` text NULL
- `industrie` text NULL
- `homepage` text NULL
- `stadt` text NULL
- `land_region` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_opportunities  (625 rows)

Columns:
- `opp_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `titel` text NULL
- `vertriebsphase` text NULL
- `zieldatum` text NULL
- `auftragswert` text NULL
- `waehrungscode` text NULL
- `kunden_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_projekte  (342 rows)

Columns:
- `projekt_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `projektname` text NULL
- `projektstatus` text NULL
- `go_live_datum` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `opp_kennung_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)
- `Region__c` text
- `Industry` text
- `Website` text
- `BillingCity` text
- `BillingCountry` text
- `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Contact
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `FirstName` text
- `LastName` text NOT NULL
- `Email` text
- `Phone` text
- `Title` text
- `Role__c` text in (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor)
- `Preferred_Language__c` text in (DE, EN, FR, ES, IT)
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Opportunity
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `StageName` text NOT NULL in (Prospecting, Qualification, Needs Analysis, Value Proposition, Id. Decision Makers, Perception Analysis, Proposal/Price Quote, Negotiation/Review, Closed Won, Closed Lost)
- `CloseDate` text NOT NULL
- `Amount` double precision
- `CurrencyIsoCode` text
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Project__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Project_Status__c` text in (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled)
- `Go_Live_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Opportunity__c` text
- `Legacy_Project_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Installed_Asset__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Serial_Number__c` text
- `Warranty_End_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text
- `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Produce the dbt project that transforms the source into the target.

Self-correction is ON: write complete models; the orchestrator will run dbt after you finish and prompt you to fix failures if needed.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 178867, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 179014, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f0f14-3c12-759d-8f76-04bb37509b7d', conversation_id='019f0f14-3c12-759d-8f76-04ba2cd5cf4f')

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    kundennummer AS "Id",

    erp_nr AS "ERP_Number__c",

        WHEN LOWER(kundenklasse) = 'silver' THEN 'Silver'

        WHEN LOWER(kundenklasse) IN ('platinum', 'platin') THEN 'Platinum'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Customer_Tier__c",
    vertriebsgebiet AS "Region__c",
    industrie AS "Industry",
    homepage AS "Website",
    stadt AS "BillingCity",
    land_region AS "BillingCountry",
    kundennummer AS "Legacy_Customer_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kunden') }}
'}, tool_call_id='pyd_ai_3ca4af5e065c4fb7a8d4a28ea8927f7c', provider_name='google-cloud', provider_details={'thought_signature': ''})], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=3405, cache_read_tokens=2578, output_tokens=403, details={'cached_content_tokens': 2578, 'thoughts_tokens': 140, 'text_prompt_tokens': 3405, 'text_cache_tokens': 2578, 'text_candidates_tokens': 263}), model_name='gemini-2.5-flash', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 41, 895844, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='google-cloud', provider_url='https://aiplatform.googleapis.com/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'STOP', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 38, 82590, tzinfo=TzInfo(0)), 'traffic_type': 'ON_DEMAND'}, provider_response_id='4kxBap6FBeu5vdIP6MXqiQE', finish_reason='stop', run_id='019f0f14-3c12-759d-8f76-04bb37509b7d', conversation_id='019f0f14-3c12-759d-8f76-04ba2cd5cf4f')

ModelResponse(parts=[ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args={'content': '-- models/Contact.sql

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    kontakt_id AS "Id",
    rufname AS "FirstName",
    COALESCE(familienname, 'Unknown') AS "LastName",
    kontakt_email AS "Email",
    tel AS "Phone",
    berufsbezeichnung AS "Title",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(rolle) IN ('decision maker', 'entscheider') THEN 'Decision Maker'
        WHEN LOWER(rolle) = 'end user' THEN 'End User'
        WHEN LOWER(rolle) = 'technical contact' THEN 'Technical Contact'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Role__c",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(korrespondenzsprache) IN ('de', 'deutsch') THEN 'DE'
        WHEN LOWER(korrespondenzsprache) IN ('en', 'english', 'englisch') THEN 'EN'
        WHEN LOWER(korrespondenzsprache) = 'fr' THEN 'FR'
        WHEN LOWER(korrespondenzsprache) = 'es' THEN 'ES'

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 178862, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_master_src.master_assets  (900 rows)

Columns:
- `asset_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `asset_name` text NULL
- `serien_nummer` text NULL
- `garantieende` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `projekt_kennung` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_kontakte  (497 rows)

Columns:
- `kontakt_id` text NOT NULL
- `rufname` text NULL
- `familienname` text NULL
- `kontakt_email` text NULL
- `tel` text NULL
- `berufsbezeichnung` text NULL
- `rolle` text NULL
- `korrespondenzsprache` text NULL
- `kd_nummer` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_kunden  (210 rows)

Columns:
- `kundennummer` text NOT NULL
- `unternehmensname` text NULL
- `erp_nr` text NULL
- `kundenklasse` text NULL
- `vertriebsgebiet` text NULL
- `industrie` text NULL
- `homepage` text NULL
- `stadt` text NULL
- `land_region` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_opportunities  (625 rows)

Columns:
- `opp_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `titel` text NULL
- `vertriebsphase` text NULL
- `zieldatum` text NULL
- `auftragswert` text NULL
- `waehrungscode` text NULL
- `kunden_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_projekte  (342 rows)

Columns:
- `projekt_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `projektname` text NULL
- `projektstatus` text NULL
- `go_live_datum` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `opp_kennung_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)
- `Region__c` text
- `Industry` text
- `Website` text
- `BillingCity` text
- `BillingCountry` text
- `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Contact
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `FirstName` text
- `LastName` text NOT NULL
- `Email` text
- `Phone` text
- `Title` text
- `Role__c` text in (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor)
- `Preferred_Language__c` text in (DE, EN, FR, ES, IT)
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Opportunity
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `StageName` text NOT NULL in (Prospecting, Qualification, Needs Analysis, Value Proposition, Id. Decision Makers, Perception Analysis, Proposal/Price Quote, Negotiation/Review, Closed Won, Closed Lost)
- `CloseDate` text NOT NULL
- `Amount` double precision
- `CurrencyIsoCode` text
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Project__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Project_Status__c` text in (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled)
- `Go_Live_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Opportunity__c` text
- `Legacy_Project_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Installed_Asset__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Serial_Number__c` text
- `Warranty_End_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text
- `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Produce the dbt project that transforms the source into the target.

Self-correction is ON: write complete models; the orchestrator will run dbt after you finish and prompt you to fix failures if needed.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 178867, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 179014, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f0f14-3c12-759d-8f76-04bb37509b7d', conversation_id='019f0f14-3c12-759d-8f76-04ba2cd5cf4f')

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    kundennummer AS "Id",
    COALESCE(unternehmensname, kundennummer) AS "Name",
    erp_nr AS "ERP_Number__c",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(kundenklasse) = 'gold' THEN 'Gold'
        WHEN LOWER(kundenklasse) = 'silver' THEN 'Silver'
        WHEN LOWER(kundenklasse) = 'bronze' THEN 'Bronze'
        WHEN LOWER(kundenklasse) IN ('platinum', 'platin') THEN 'Platinum'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Customer_Tier__c",
    vertriebsgebiet AS "Region__c",
    industrie AS "Industry",
    homepage AS "Website",
    stadt AS "BillingCity",
    land_region AS "BillingCountry",
    kundennummer AS "Legacy_Customer_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kunden') }}

ModelResponse(parts=[ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args={'content': '-- models/Opportunity.sql

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    opp_kennung AS "Id",
    COALESCE(titel, opp_kennung) AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(vertriebsphase) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect', 'in kontakt') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(vertriebsphase) IN ('qualification', 'qualifikation', 'quali', 'in prüfung') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(vertriebsphase) IN ('won', 'closed won', 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)', 'gewonnen', 'closed won') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(vertriebsphase) IN ('lost', 'verloren', 'closed lost', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE 'Prospecting' -- Default for NOT NULL StageName
    END AS "StageName",
    COALESCE(
        TO_CHAR(CAST(zieldatum AS DATE), 'YYYY-MM-DD'), -- YYYY-MM-DD
        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD'), -- DD.MM.YYYY
        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD'), -- M/D/YYYY
        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD'), -- YYYYMMDD
        TO_CHAR(CURRENT_DATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD') -- Fallback for NOT NULL CloseDate
    ) AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN auftragswert IS NULL THEN NULL
        ELSE
            CAST(REPLACE(
                REPLACE(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, '[^0-9,.-]', '', 'g'),
                    '.', '
                )
            , ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
    END AS "Amount",
    waehrungscode AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    kunden_ref AS "AccountId",
    opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_opportunities') }}
', 'path': 'models/Opportunity.sql'}, tool_call_id='pyd_ai_d4e95c16eb0f4b5ebe069c029334741e', provider_name='google-cloud', provider_details={'thought_signature': ''})], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=6300, cache_read_tokens=5158, output_tokens=1945, details={'cached_content_tokens': 5158, 'thoughts_tokens': 1402, 'text_prompt_tokens': 6300, 'text_cache_tokens': 5158, 'text_candidates_tokens': 543}), model_name='gemini-2.5-flash', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 34, 8, 402732, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='google-cloud', provider_url='https://aiplatform.googleapis.com/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'STOP', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 53, 933065, tzinfo=TzInfo(0)), 'traffic_type': 'ON_DEMAND'}, provider_response_id='8UxBasn5OJG928oPiO262Q8', finish_reason='stop')

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 16, 33, 32, 178862, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_master_src.master_assets  (900 rows)

Columns:
- `asset_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `asset_name` text NULL
- `serien_nummer` text NULL
- `garantieende` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `projekt_kennung` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

- `rolle` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_src.master_opportunities  (625 rows)

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)
- `Region__c` text
